items.title: Permanent posts for all housemen by 2028 under MOH reforms
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Health Ministry (MOH) targets to offer permanent posts to all housemen immediately upon completing their training by 2028, said Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad. "By 2028, MOH targets to offer permanent posts to all housemen immediately upon completing their training. "This year alone, 4,500 contract medical officers will be absorbed into permanent roles, alongside 800 new positions approved annually," he said.

items.title: SD Property expands urban redevelopment play with RM160mil Unirazak deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Sime Darby Property Bhd (SD Property) is acquiring Wisma Universiti Tun Abdul Razak (Unirazak) here from Permodalan Nasional Bhd (PNB) for RM160 million as part of its strategy to expand its presence in urban redevelopment projects. In a Bursa Malaysia filing today, the property developer said its wholly-owned Sime Darby Property (KLGCC Resort) Sdn Bhd had accepted a binding letter of offer from PNB. SD Property group managing director and chief executive officer Datuk Seri Azmir Merican said the acquisition positions the group to deliver a landmark development befitting the site's prime location while strengthening its presence in the premium property segment.

items.title: Johor polls: Pakatan’s Pasir Raja candidate courts youth and Felda settlers using hybrid approach
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 6 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Pasir Raja state constituency, Mohd Fakharuddin Moslim is using a so-called hybrid strategy to win over voters with just four days left before polling day for the Johor state election. He said the hybrid approach combined aggressive face-to-face campaigning on the ground with dominance of narratives on social media platforms, a method that proved effective as his machinery successfully completed a 100 per cent tour across all localities in the state constituency. In terms of physical coverage, our machinery had successfully traced the entire Pasir Raja state constituency, including relatively remote areas such as Sungai Redan.

items.title: Woman fined RM500 for assaulting Sultan of Perak
items.summary: IPOH: A woman was fined RM500 by the magistrate's court here today, after pleading guilty to using criminal force against the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah, during the state's National Day celebration last year. The single mother of five was charged with using criminal force against Sultan Nazrin at 8.20am on Aug 31, 2025, in front of the Ipoh City Council building on Jalan Panglima Bukit Gantang. According to the facts of the case, Nurhaswani climbed onto the royal dais during the Perak National Day celebration and rushed towards Sultan Nazrin while the Perak state anthem was being played.

items.title: Dewan Rakyat passes bill to strengthen MyCC powers to tackle cartels and monopolies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — The Dewan Rakyat today passed the Competition Commission (Amendment) Bill 2026, aimed at strengthening the Malaysia Competition Commission (MyCC) and ensuring comprehensive enforcement of the country’s competition law framework. In his winding-up speech, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the bill, which contained 34 amendments, was important in strengthening a fair, transparent and dynamic market amid increasingly complex cartel activities. Through the latest amendments, he said MyCC’s powers to require the provision of information had been extended to include the implementation of market reviews.

items.title: Johor campaign debate risks rhetoric over policy, group says
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor Writers' Association has urged political parties to set aside calls for a debate during the Johor election campaign. Its head, Amiruddin Ali Hanafiah, said such a debate would likely be dominated by political rhetoric rather than meaningful policy discussions. Amiruddin said any debate should be held after the election, when discussions could focus on governance, policy implementation and issues affecting the people.

items.title: Asdion shares to be suspended July 14, faces delisting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Asdion Bhd's securities will be suspended from trading on July 14, with the company set to be delisted from Bursa Malaysia's ACE Market on July 16 after it failed to appoint a replacement sponsor for its Guidance Note 3 (GN3) regularisation plan following the resignation of Kenanga Investment Bank Bhd. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, it was stated that the software developer failed to appoint a replacement sponsor after Kenanga ceased acting as its principal adviser and sponsor, effective immediately. "If the company submits an appeal to Bursa Securities within the appeal timeframe, the de-listing will be deferred pending the appeal decision, although trading in the company's securities will remain suspended from July 14," said Bursa Malaysia.

items.title: Death of tahfiz student Wan Ahmad Faris classified as NFA
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Attorney-General's Chambers (A-GC) has announced that the death of tahfiz student Wan Ahmad Faris Wan Abdul Rahman, who was found dead and believed to have been murdered in 2013, has been classified as no further action. Attorney-General Tan Sri Mohd Dusuki Mokhtar said a thorough investigation revealed no evidence linking any other individual to the deceased's death. "The results of the comprehensive investigation found no evidence that could link any other individual to the incident of the deceased's death," he said.

items.title: MARKET PULSE PM JULY 6, 2026 [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia extended its gains on Monday to close higher at 1,683 points. Plantation and healthcare stocks led the advance, with plantation counters buoyed by renewed interest. Healthcare stocks also gained as investors sought their defensive earnings profile.

items.title: Hamas announces dissolution of Gaza governing body
items.summary: GAZA CITY: Palestinian resistance movement Hamas announced Monday the dissolution of the body that has governed the Gaza Strip for nearly two decades, clearing the way for a technocratic committee to implement civilian rule. The move marks a significant political shift by Hamas, which has run Gaza since its fighters seized control from rival Palestinian movement Fatah in 2007 after winning legislative elections the previous year. "The head of the government's emergency committee Mohammed al-Farra has officially submitted his resignation," Ismail al-Thawabta, head of Hamas' government media office told AFP.

items.title: Ringgit ends lower as investors return to US dollar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed lower against the US dollar on Monday as investors returned to the greenback following its recent pullback after a weaker-than-expected US June employment report. Investors also remained cautious ahead of the release of the US Federal Reserve's June meeting minutes this week, said Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid. He said other Asian currencies, like the ringgit, were also generally weaker against the US dollar.

items.title: EU keen to expand strategic cooperation with Terengganu
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: The European Union (EU) is keen to expand cooperation with Terengganu in strategic sectors, including the green economy, digitalisation, Artificial Intelligence (AI), and Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET), following discussions with the state leadership today. EU Ambassador to Malaysia Rafael Daerr said the three-day visit by EU ambassadors and representatives to the state, beginning Sunday, aimed to better understand Terengganu's development agenda and identify new areas of collaboration. He said Terengganu offered significant opportunities for European investment, noting that several European companies are already operating in the state and that more than 2,000 European companies have operations across Malaysia.

items.title: Health minister aims for all housemen to get permanent posts by 2028
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 6 — The Health Ministry (MOH) targets to offer permanent posts to all housemen immediately upon completing their training by 2028, said Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad. “By 2028, MOH targets to offer permanent posts to all housemen immediately upon completing their training. This year alone, 4,500 contract medical officers will be absorbed into permanent roles, alongside 800 new positions approved annually,” he said.

items.title: Banting school stabbing: Focus on school security, mental health support
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government must take immediate, holistic action to address security gaps in schools and the rising wave of youth mental health issues, said Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki. "The government needs to take immediate and holistic action to address safety issues in schools, alongside the rising number of cases involving mental health," the Umno secretary-general said in a Facebook post. The Education Ministry is prioritising medical care and psychosocial support for the school community following the incident.

items.title: Mat Sabu to voters: Ignore Pas directives, they change with political convenience
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu has urged voters to disregard political directives or "fatwas" issued by Pas, claiming they change according to the party's political interests rather than consistent principles. "Even if Pas is strong, don't worry. Don't follow Pas' fatwas.

items.title: BM GreenTech expands renewable portfolio with NVP acquisition
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: BM GreenTech Bhd's unit has bought a 100 per cent stake in Nefin V Power Sdn Bhd (NVP), a special purpose vehicle with rights to develop a 29.99 megawatt alternating current solar photovoltaic (PV) farm under the Corporate Green Power Programme. The acquisition was undertaken through BM GreenTech's subsidiary Plus Xnergy Holding Sdn Bhd. "The first SPA involves the acquisition of 900,000 ordinary shares from Nefin Energy (Malaysia) Sdn Bhd, representing 90 per cent of NVP's total issued and paid-up share capital, for RM3.24 million.

items.title: WCT Holdings accepts RM926.2mil construction contract in Abu Dhabi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: WCT Holdings Bhd's wholly-owned subsidiary has accepted a RM926.20 million construction sub-contract in Abu Dhabi, the United Arab Emirates, through a 50:50 joint venture (JV) with Construction General Contracting House Ltd (CGCH). In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, WCT said WCT International Bhd and UAE-incorporated CGCH, through their 50:50 yet-to-be-incorporated joint venture, accepted the sub-contract letter of award issued by CGCH. "Following the acceptance of the sub-contract, WCT International Sdn Bhd and CGCH shall, as soon as reasonably practicable, either directly or through their respective wholly owned subsidiaries, incorporate the JV company," the filing said.

items.title: Enest IPO oversubscribed 1.94 times ahead of ACE Market listing
items.summary: The pure bird's nest supplier said 2,871 applications for 85.33 million new shares worth RM11.12 million were received from the Malaysian public. "For the Bumiputera portion, a total of 1,474 applications for 29.8 million new shares were received, which represents an oversubscription rate of 1.05 times. "The 20.87 million shares (comprising 5.81 million issue shares and 15.05 million offer shares) by way of private placement to selected investors have been fully placed out," it said.

items.title: Daddy issues? Nato's Rutte sticks to charm to keep Trump on side
items.summary: WHEN Nato chief Mark Rutte travelled to Washington ahead of this year's Ankara summit he had a special weapon to please Donald Trump: a gold-lettered presentation on what a great job the US president has done for the alliance. The move, designed to highlight how Europe is ramping up defence spending under pressure from Trump, was the latest leg of Rutte's relentless charm offensive to keep the mercurial US leader on board through a rollercoaster of crises. "Rutte is one of the last men standing in Europe who still has a relationship with Trump," former senior Nato official Jamie Shea, now with Chatham House think tank, told AFP.

items.title: Omoda/Jaecoo expands retail footprint with new Selayang 3S entre
items.summary: Omoda/Jaecco Malaysia has strengthened its nationwide retail and aftersales presence with the official opening of a new 3S (sales, service and spare parts) centre in Selayang, operated by Carro Digital Sdn Bhd. Head of Omoda/Jaecoo Malaysia Chris Tan said the new outlet reflects the brand's confidence in the Malaysian market and its commitment to growing alongside trusted dealer partners. The new facility follows Carro Digital's appointment as an authorised Omoda/Jaecoo dealer in March 2026 and extends the brand's reach to customers in Selayang, Batu Caves, Templer, Rawang and surrounding areas.

items.title: KLIA touts: RTD seizes 54 vehicles in two-week crackdown
items.summary: SEPANG: The Road Transport Department (RTD) has seized 54 vehicles in a two-week special operation targeting illegal airport taxi touts, or ulat, operating at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminals 1 and 2. "The seized vehicles comprised 27 private cars, two company-owned vehicles, nine taxis and 16 e-hailing vehicles," he said. Aedy said that since January, RTD has inspected 151 vehicles linked to illegal taxi activities, taken enforcement action against 131 vehicles and seized 133 vehicles.

items.title: 143 e-waste containers removed in Port Klang operation
items.summary: KLANG: Authorities have removed 143 containers carrying about 3,060 tonnes of electronic waste from Malaysia under the first phase of an enforcement operation at Port Klang, a senior official said today. Port Klang Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said the containers were removed as part of efforts to curb the illegal importation of electronic waste. He said enforcement personnel had so far inspected 685 containers under the operation.

items.title: Banting school stabbing: Student stable after hospital treatment
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 6 — A student injured in a stabbing incident at a secondary school in Banting, Kuala Langat, is in stable condition after receiving emergency treatment at Hospital Banting, the Selangor Health Department said today. In a statement, the department said the student arrived at the hospital’s Emergency Department at 10.13am and was immediately attended to by the medical team. “The victim was given emergency treatment without delay.

items.title: Trump goads Italy's Meloni again on eve of Nato summit
items.summary: ROME: Italy's defence minister stressed the importance of good transatlantic relations on Monday after US President Donald Trump again goaded erstwhile ally Giorgia Meloni, the Italian prime minister. Meloni last month accused Trump of fabricating a story about her after the US president told an Italian TV channel that she had "begged" him to take a photo with her at a G7 summit in France. The Italian prime minister has yet to respond.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends higher lifted by banking, plantation stocks
items.summary: Turnover decreased to 2.68 billion units valued at RM1.69 billion from 2.79 billion units valued at RM1.93 billion last Friday. On the most active list, HHRG ticked up one sen to nine sen, ECKEM Holdings gained 1.5 sen to 13.5 sen, Tanco perked up one sen to 18.5 sen, while Silver Ridge slipped 2.5 sen to 18.5 sen, and Zetrix AI slid three sen to 76 sen. As for the top gainers, United Plantations garnered 38 sen to RM33.50, Petronas Dagangan added 28 sen to RM19.54, Bintulu Port soared 21 sen to RM5.56, Hong Leong Financial climbed 18 sen to RM18.68, and Hong Leong Industries increased 14 sen to RM18.82. Warrants turnover expanded to 1.04 billion units worth RM141.68 million versus 854.95 million units worth RM106.34 million previously.

items.title: Permaju unit secures Chery's brand Lepas dealership in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Permaju Industries Bhd's wholly-owned subsidiary Cergaz Autohaus Sdn Bhd has secured a dealer agreement to sell Lepas vehicles in Malaysia. In a Bursa Malaysia filing, Permaju said Cergaz Autohaus had entered into an agreement with Chery Auto Malaysia Sdn Bhd to be a non-exclusive authorised dealer for the Lepas brand within an assigned market area. Permaju said the dealer agreement enables the company through its unit to expand its automotive business by becoming an authorised dealer for Lepas vehicles in Malaysia.

items.title: Johor election: Police, military personnel free to decide who they want to vote for
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan has rejected claims that there is any manipulation in determining the votes of police and military personnel in the 16th Johor election. Also present was BN candidate for Kempas, Datuk Ramlee Bohani. Ahead of early voting tomorrow, Mohamad said he hopes that all police and military personnel in the state will join other Johoreans in maintaining BN's mandate to govern the state.

items.title: Storm alert for Selangor, KL, Johor, other states until 9PM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit eight states and Kuala Lumpur until 9 pm tonight. According to a warning issued by the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) this evening, the states affected are Perak (Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Mualim) and Kelantan (Jeli and Kuala Krai). Similar conditions are also expected to hit Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Raub, Bentong and Rompin), Selangor (Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat), Negeri Sembilan (Seremban and Jelebu) and Johor (Segamat, Kluang, Mersing, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru).

items.title: Maybank IBG lifts Malaysia's 2026 GDP forecast to 4.9pct
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Maybank Investment Banking Group (Maybank IBG) has raised its 2026 gross domestic product (GDP) growth forecast to 4.9 per cent from 4.4 per cent previously. According to Maybank IBG, economic data for April and May suggest another quarter of growth exceeding five per cent. Maybank IBG maintained its positive outlook on the domestic equity market, keeping its year-end target for the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI at 1,750.

items.title: Johor election: Focus on PH ability to deliver manifesto promises, says Mat Sabu
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu has dismissed claims that the Pakatan Harapan Johor election manifesto is a "copy-paste" document. Mohamad, better known as Mat Sabu, also expressed confidence that PH would be able to form the Johor government after polling on July 11. On July 3, PH launched its "Johor Untuk Semua" manifesto as its offer to voters in conjunction with the 16th Johor state election.

items.title: Three arrested for robbing migrant worker while posing as police in Manjung
items.summary: IPOH, July 6 — Police have busted a robbery syndicate that impersonated policemen with the arrest of three local men within hours of their alleged robbery of a Bangladeshi man in Manjung last Thursday. Perak Police Chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the 45-year-old victim lodged a police report at 8.06 pm, claiming he had been robbed by two local men who identified themselves as policemen in an incident at Pantai Remis at about 4.30 pm the same day. “The suspects had threatened to take the victim to the Pantai Remis Police Station for further inspection.

items.title: Police bust RM3.25mil illegal World Cup betting transactions in Op Soga XI
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have uncovered RM3.25 million in illegal betting transactions under Op Soga XI, a special operation targeting illegal sports betting syndicates capitalising on the FIFA World Cup 2026. He said police also seized RM167,062.09 in cash during the nationwide operation. Kumar said investigators also detected 263 online platforms, including websites, applications and social media accounts, used to facilitate football betting and promote gambling activities.

items.title: Fadhlina: Medical, psychosocial support top priority after school stabbing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Education Ministry is prioritising medical care and psychosocial support for the school community following a stabbing incident involving a secondary school student in Banting this morning. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek, who visited the scene earlier today, said police had taken all necessary security measures and detained the suspect. Concurrently, providing a psychosocial support system for the school community is the ministry's top priority," she said in a Facebook post.

items.title: PM calls for fair, swift resolution to Felda settlers' land, housing issues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called for a comprehensive and fair resolution to long-standing issues facing Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) settlers, particularly regarding land ownership and second-generation housing. Anwar said any resolution must be swift and centred on the community's welfare. "During today's meeting (with Felda representatives), I stressed that all long-standing issues need to be resolved comprehensively and fairly, particularly those involving second-generation housing and settlers' land ownership," he said in a statement issued after a meeting with Felda's top management at the Prime Minister's Office today.

items.title: Pas to decide Negri Sembilan seats after Johor polls, says Tuan Ibrahim
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Pas will wait for the outcome of the Johor election before deciding which seats to contest in the Negri Sembilan polls, its deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man said. Tuan Ibrahim said discussions on seat allocations for the Negri Sembilan election had been put on hold for now. We will wait for the outcome in Johor before deciding," he said.

items.title: GTA eyes Brunei expansion with Safran partnership
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Global Turbine Asia Sdn Bhd (GTA) is expanding its regional ambitions after signing a memorandum of understanding (MoU) with Safran Helicopter Engines Asia to extend their long-standing partnership into Brunei. The MoU establishes a framework for both parties to complete the qualification, approval and contractual processes required to support the proposed expansion of GTA's authorised distribution and customer support operations in Brunei. "As a trusted industrial partner of Safran Helicopter Engines, GTA continues to strengthen its capabilities and position itself to support customers across Southeast Asia through its aerospace maintenance, repair and overhaul services," it said.

items.title: Sentul swoop uncovers World Cup betting hub doubling as unlicensed entertainment outlet, 33 foreigners detained
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — Police raided a premises in Taman Wahyu, Sentul, yesterday, believed to be used as a 2026 World Cup football betting call centre as well as an unlicensed entertainment outlet, detaining 34 individuals, including 33 foreigners. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the raid was conducted at 3.30am by the Gambling, Vice and Secret Societies Division (D7) of the Sentul district police headquarters' Criminal Investigation Department, following surveillance and intelligence gathering. “Initial investigations found the premises were used to manage, organise, and promote online gambling involving 2026 World Cup football betting, besides operating as an unlicensed entertainment outlet.

items.title: Woman fined RM500 after admitting using criminal force against Perak Sultan during state-level National Day event
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — A woman who admitted using criminal force against the Sultan of Perak during last year’s state-level National Day celebrations was fined RM500 by the Magistrates’ Court in Ipoh today. According to Sinar Harian , Magistrate Norizan Riduan imposed the fine after Nurhaswani Afni Mohamad Zorki, 42, pleaded guilty to the charge. The court also ordered her to serve one month’s imprisonment if she fails to pay the fine.

items.title: PMO proposed every Jana Wibawa contractor, Tengku Zafrul tells ex-PM Muhyiddin’s trial
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — All of the Malaysian government’s projects under the Muhyiddin administration’s Jana Wibawa programme were awarded to contractors that had been proposed directly by the Prime Minister’s Office (PMO), former finance minister Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz said today. Tengku Zafrul said this while testifying as the 11th prosecution witness in former prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s power abuse and money laundering trial in connection to the Jana Wibawa project. “Throughout the Jana Wibawa programme, all contractor companies that were awarded projects are companies that had been proposed by the Prime Minister’s Office only.

items.title: Sitiawan raids reveal 208 live bullets and weapons cache as police move on trafficking suspect
items.summary: IPOH, July 6 — Police seized 208 live rounds of ammunition and several objects resembling firearms, besides arresting a local man in two raids linked to drug trafficking activities in Sitiawan, near Manjung, last Wednesday. Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the operation conducted by the Perak Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (NCID) also resulted in the seizure of drugs believed to be Methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA) and ketamine, with a total estimated value of RM6,547. He said said following the arrest, police raided a house in Jalan Kampung Cina, Sitiawan, where they found 12 transparent plastic packets containing substances believed to be ketamine, weighing 64.30 grammes.

items.title: High Court sets Oct 12 showdown as MCMC takes Papagomo to task over censorship talk
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — The High Court today fixed October 12 to hear an inter partes interim injunction application by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) against blogger Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, better known as Papagomo, in a defamation suit. The court also extends the ad interim injunction,” the judge said. Earlier, MCMC counsel Nurshafiqa Balqish Jaffri informed the court that Wan Muhammad Azri had entered an appearance in the suit.

items.title: Victim’s care, school support are top priority after Banting stabbing, says education minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the government’s immediate priority is ensuring medical treatment for the student injured in a stabbing at a secondary school in Banting, while providing psychosocial support for the wider school community as police investigate the incident. In a Facebook post after visiting the school today, Fadhlina said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) had taken security measures to bring the situation under control, including arresting the suspect. “The priority now is to ensure the victim receives medical treatment, while the Ministry of Education’s priority is to provide a psychosocial support system for the school community.

items.title: Sabah minister flags unlicensed centres, urges mums and dads to stick with approved childcare
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 6 — State Women, Health and People’s Wellbeing Minister Datuk Julita Majungki has urged parents to send their children to registered childcare centres to safeguard their safety and wellbeing, while highlighting the need to expand childcare capacity in Sabah. She said the installation of closed-circuit television (CCTV) systems is a mandatory requirement for registered childcare centres and an important measure to help ensure children's safety. Speaking at the closing ceremony of the Sabah State-Level Childcare Centre Day Celebration here on Sunday, Julita said Sabah has only about 230 registered childcare centres despite an estimated 445,000 children aged up to four by 2030 requiring around 110,000 childcare places, describing investment in early childhood development as vital for families, human capital and the State's future.

items.title: ‘Give agriculture centre stage’: DOSM chief urges stronger domestic output to secure Malaysia’s food future
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 6 — Malaysia recorded a score of 61.5 in the Malaysian Food Security Index 2024, an increase from 54.5 in 2023, indicating that the country’s food security level is on the right track. Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) chief statistician Datuk Seri Dr. Mohd Uzir Mahidin said that from the four main dimensions evaluated, accessibility recorded the highest score of 79.9, followed by stability (54.6), usability (50.4) and availability (50). He said that the high accessibility score indicates that Malaysia does not face problems in obtaining food supplies because the shortfall in domestic production can still be compensated via imports.

items.title: Umno deputy president dismisses Najib link claims, says Johor election about state government not pardons
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 6 — The Johor state election campaign must be conducted maturely without personal attacks to ensure national stability is preserved, said Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan. “Let us not be overly personal. Our parties are fine, nothing personal.

items.title: DOSM: Govt to develop Malaysia’s first cost-of-living benchmark for families caring for disabled and elderly dependants
items.summary: According to Sinar Harian , the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) is developing a Basic Expenditure for Decent Living (PAKW) benchmark tailored to households with special care needs, the first of its kind in the country. Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the initiative would measure the actual cost of living borne by these households while serving as an important reference for the government in designing financial assistance, subsidies, caregiving support and social protection programmes. “It also includes patients requiring home-based care, whether bedridden or not, as well as older persons who require specialised care,” Mohd Uzir said.

items.title: Witness: Schoolgirl stabbed after buying bread at canteen during Banting school recess
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — A routine morning recess at a secondary school in Banting was abruptly interrupted when a Form Three student was allegedly stabbed moments after buying bread at the school canteen. A male student who was outside the school after the incident told the national daily that the suspect had approached the canteen from the direction of the school’s surau shortly before the attack. “The incident happened during recess for Form Three students.

items.title: Malaysia eyes overland durian exports to China through Thailand, says Mat Sabu
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 6 — Malaysia is in talks with the Thai government and the General Administration of Customs of China (GACC) to establish a land route for exporting local durians to China, a move expected to reduce logistics costs and expand market access in the country. Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said the initiative is part of the government’s efforts to support durian growers, particularly amid a current glut following the durian season, which has occurred simultaneously in several states. On food security, Mohamad said Malaysia still needs to raise its self-sufficiency levels for several key commodities, including rice, meat, and maize for animal feed.

items.title: Banting schoolgirl stabbed up to 16 times, suffered punctured lung, father says
items.summary: KUALA KUMPUR, July 6 — A schoolgirl injured in a stabbing at a secondary school in Banting was stabbed up to 16 times and suffered a punctured lung, her father said today, as police continued investigating the attack. Online , the victim’s father, Mohd Firuz Ibrahim, 43, said he received a call from one of his daughter’s teachers at about 9.30am before rushing to Hospital Banting, where she is being treated. Mohd Firuz said most of the wounds were to his daughter’s back.

items.title: Customs officer pleads not guilty to RM15,000 bribery charge in Shah Alam court
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 6 — An assistant director with the Royal Malaysian Customs Department pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to receiving RM15,000 in bribes last month. MACC Deputy Public Prosecutor Muaz Ahmad Khairuddin proposed bail of RM10,000, subject to additional conditions that the accused surrender his passport to the court, report to a nearby MACC office and refrain from contacting prosecution witnesses. Lawyer Azrul Zulkifli Stork, representing Fareez Akmal, asked the court to consider bail of RM5,000, arguing that his client supports a family and elderly parents in poor health.

items.title: MB: Perak to review Sejahtera 2030 blueprint as state updates development agenda
items.summary: IPOH, July 6 — The Perak government will carry out a mid-term review of its Perak Sejahtera 2030 blueprint to ensure the state's long-term development agenda remains relevant and on track to achieve its 2030 goals. Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said the review would include refining monitoring indicators, strengthening the blueprint's agenda and flagship initiatives, as well as updating the Perak Sejahtera 2030 Dashboard. Saarani said the review was intended to ensure the state's development blueprint remained dynamic and responsive to changing economic conditions and development needs.

items.title: Malaysia, Thailand to tackle trade, logistics and border issues at September talks, says foreign minister
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 6 — The Malaysia-Thailand Annual Dialogue, scheduled to take place in the country in September, will focus on the agenda of trade relations, logistics coordination, and finalising land border issues. Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said the annual platform will be fully utilised to resolve several outstanding issues that require coordination at the level of both countries. There is also the border issue that we need to resolve, with only a little left regarding the land border issue.

items.title: Selangor Education Dept says Banting school stabbing suspect in police custody, victim remains stable
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — The suspect involved in the stabbing of a schoolgirl at a school in Banting is currently in police custody, the Selangor Education Department said today. Confirming the incident, the department said it is extending its full cooperation to the authorities in the ongoing investigation. Earlier, Kuala Langat police chief Superintendent Mohd Akmalrizal Radzi confirmed the incident when contacted, adding that the victim is in stable condition.

items.title: RM81.8m bird’s nest bust marks biggest success in Sarawak wildlife crackdown
items.summary: KUCHING, July 6 — The integrated Operasi Bersepadu Khazanah (OBK) in Sarawak recorded its biggest success with the seizure of assets worth RM81.8 million involving the illegal trade of bird’s nests during a series of operations conducted from June 1 to 16. Bukit Aman Internal Security and Public Order Department (JKDNKA) director Datuk Seri Mohd Yusri Hassan Basri said six individuals were arrested in 10 raids, effectively crippling the illegal processing and commercial trade of bird’s nests in the Kuching area. Among the items seized during the operation were 16,367 kilogrammes of bird’s nests; 223 birds of various species; eight eagles; two pangolins; 790 turtle eggs; 4,238 tortoises of various species; 218 axolotl salamanders; 116 exotic fish; 959 fighting cocks; 1,673 logs; 7,600 saplings of various species; 14 animal microchips; and nine mammals of various species.

items.title: Woman killed, two drivers injured in two-car crash in Miri
items.summary: MIRI, July 6 — An elderly woman died and two men injured in an accident involving two vehicles at Jalan Morsjaya here. Bomba reported that the injured MPV driver managed to exit the vehicle on his own. “The passenger, an elderly woman, 78, remained trapped inside the vehicle.

items.title: Police hunt suspect after woman allegedly assaulted outside Kuching rental room
items.summary: KUCHING, July 6 — Police are investigating an incident involving a man who allegedly assaulted a woman in front of a rental room at Metrocity Matang here last Friday. Kuching district police chief ACP Alexson Naga Chabu confirmed that a report on the incident, which was said to have occurred at around 8pm, was received the following day. Police appealed for anyone with information related to the incident to come forward to assist by contacting the Kuching District Police Headquarters or the investigating officer Sgt Mancha, at 019-5958260.

items.title: With over half of voters under 39, Pakatan’s Pasir Raja hopeful says youth can shape Johor’s future
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 6 — Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) Pasir Raja candidate believes the constituency’s large youth electorate, including first-time voters, could play a decisive role in shaping Johor’s future at the upcoming state election. Fakharuddin Moslim said young voters form a distinct demographic in Pasir Raja, with 54 per cent of eligible voters aged below 39. Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) Pasir Raja candidate Fakharuddin Moslim (right) with a Kampung Sungai Telor resident during his campaign trail in the Chinese areas of the Pasir Raja state constituency today.

items.title: Parliament hears 18 baby abandonment cases reported across Malaysia in first three months of 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — Malaysia recorded 18 baby abandonment cases nationwide between January and March this year, compared with 72 cases reported throughout 2025, Deputy Women, Family and Community Development Minister Lim Hui Ying told Parliament today. According to Astro Awani , Lim said Selangor accounted for 10 of the 18 reported cases, followed by Sabah and Penang with two each. “In addressing the issue of baby abandonment, the Ministry of Women, Family and Community Development, through its agencies namely the Social Welfare Department (JKM) and the National Population and Family Development Board (LPPKN), implements various approaches covering prevention, protection and intervention,” Lim said during Question Time in the Dewan Rakyat.

items.title: ‘No more queues, no more congestion’: Ministry abolishes case‑by‑case approvals for foreign worker quotas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — The Human Resources Ministry (Kesuma) today abolished the previous case-by-case process for foreign worker quota applications, stating all requests must now be submitted and processed through a centralised online system as part of a wider overhaul of the approval process. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the move follows the Cabinet’s decision to place the One Stop Centre (OSC) for foreign worker management under Kesuma, with applications to be handled through the Foreign Workers Centralised Management System (FWCMS). To illustrate how the new system differs from the previous process, Ramanan gave the example of an Indian restaurant owner seeking to hire foreign workers.

items.title: FAM awaits full report after player killed in suspected lightning strike during Melaka friendly
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — The Football Association of Malaysia (FAM) will get a full report from the Melaka Football Association (MFA) and the match organiser following an incident in which a player died after he was believed to have been struck by lightning during a friendly in Melaka yesterday. FAM secretary-general Datuk Noor Azman Rahman said the report will review the chronology of the incident and the actions taken, before identifying areas for improvement in game safety management. He said FAM also encourages organisers to consider using a Lightning Detection System at competition venues.
